name: Gamma Scalping (Long Gamma / Delta-Neutral)
description: Buy at-the-money options (long gamma) and continuously delta-hedge the position to remain directionally neutral. Each hedge rebalance locks in a small profit when the stock moves. The strategy profits when realized volatility exceeds the implied vol paid for the options.
core_logic: Buy ATM straddle. Delta_t = dP/dS ≈ 0 (neutral). When stock moves ΔS, re-hedge. P&L per hedge = 0.5 * Gamma * ΔS^2 - Theta * dt. Net P&L > 0 when RV > IV.

sharpe_ratio.note: depends heavily on IV paid vs RV realized

risk.main_risk: Theta decay when market is quiet; overpaying for IV

pros: Positive crisis alpha when vol spikes
cons: Theta decay burns premium in quiet markets
Must overpay IV to enter position
signal_buy: Short-term RV > 21-day RV by >20% (volatility burst → long gamma profitable)
signal_sell: Short-term RV < 21-day RV by >30% (quiet market → theta decay dominates)
